perturbation.original_text: Except as expressly authorized by prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (a) limit access to any Confidential Information received by it solely to its Representatives who have a need to know such in connection with any current or future negotiations or arrangements between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party, including, without limitation in connection with the membership of the Investor in the Company and any commercial arrangements between the Investor and the Company and only for use in connection therewith. The Investor may provide access to and may disclose Confidential Information of the Company to any other Member of the Company in connection with such Member's participation as a Member of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as expressly authorized by prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (a) limit access to any Confidential Information received by it solely to its Representatives who have a need to know such in connection with current negotiations or arrangements between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party, including, without limitation in connection with the membership of the Investor in the Company and any commercial arrangements between the Investor and the Company and only for use in connection therewith. The Investor may provide access to and may disclose Confidential Information of the Company to any other Member of the Company in connection with such Member's participation as a Member of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'future' negotiations or arrangements from section 2, it suggests that the Receiving Party only needs to limit access of confidential information to representatives involved in current negotiations. This contradicts the survival clause in section 6, as it implies there is no need to keep information confidential for potential future negotiations after the termination of the agreement. This creates uncertainty whether the obligation to confidentiality extends to information relevant to future negotiations after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may not be terminated except following Withdrawal by the Investor pursuant to the terms of the LLC Agreement. The obligations of confidentiality and limitations on use under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the execution of this Agreement; PROVIDED, CONFIDENTIAL Page 29 <Page> HOWEVER, notwithstanding the term of this Agreement, any provisions hereof that by their nature extend beyond such term or termination shall remain in effect beyond such term or termination under this Section 6 until fulfilled and shall apply to either Party's successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'future negotiations or arrangements', while the changed text omits 'future', narrowing the scope to only 'current negotiations'. This directly contradicts the survival clause in the 'Contradicted Text' section, which states that confidentiality obligations survive termination of the agreement for five years and apply to provisions that 'by their nature extend beyond such term or termination'. By limiting the confidentiality to current negotiations, the changed text implies that confidentiality may not extend to future negotiations after termination, creating a strong contradiction with the survival clause's intent to protect confidential information even after the agreement ends.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall (i) destroy all memoranda, notes, records, drawings, manuals, other documents or materials and any other manifestation (and all copies thereof) pertaining to or arising from the Confidential Information in any form or medium (whether written, typewritten, recorded, electronic or otherwise) and (ii) at the option of the Receiving Party, either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy the Confidential Information supplied by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives. Upon the return and/or destruction of such materials, the Receiving Party agrees to certify, in writing, that all of the foregoing materials have been destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall (i) destroy all memoranda, notes, records, drawings, manuals, other documents or materials and any other manifestation (and all copies thereof) pertaining to or arising from the Confidential Information in any form or medium (whether written, typewritten, recorded, electronic or otherwise). Upon the return and/or destruction of such materials, the Receiving Party agrees to certify, in writing, that all of the foregoing materials have been destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3 states that upon request, the Receiving Party shall destroy all confidential information related materials and (ii) at the *option of the Receiving Party*, return OR destroy confidential information supplied by the Disclosing Party. The modification removes the option of returning or destroying. This in effect forces the receiving party to destroy the originals, rather than return them. This contradicts section 5, which states that title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party. Making it obligatory to destroy original confidential information undermines the ownership rights of the disclosing party, and that the other party can't have any interest.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does not confer any right, license, interest or title in, to or under the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. No license is hereby granted to the Receiving Party, by estoppel or otherwise, under any patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights of the Disclosing Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Receiving Party the option to either return or destroy the Confidential Information. The changed text removes this option, forcing the Receiving Party to destroy the information, which contradicts the Contradicted Text stating that Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party. The modification effectively undermines the Disclosing Party's ownership by making destruction obligatory, causing a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The obligation of confidentiality and restriction on use in Section 2 hereof shall not apply to any Confidential Information that the Receiving Party proves: (a) was in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement or subsequently came into the public domain other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party; (b) was received by the Receiving Party from a third party free of any obligation of confidence of the Receiving Party to such third party; provided that such third party is not known by the Receiving Party, after reasonable diligence, to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other obligation of secrecy to the Disclosing Party; (c) was already in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party, without obligation of confidentiality, prior to receipt thereof, directly or indirectly, from the Disclosing Party; (d) is requested to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative law, regulation or proceeding after all reasonable legal remedies for maintaining such information in confidence have been exhausted, including, but not limited to, giving the Disclosing Party reasonable advance notice of the potential for such disclosure and allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order concerning such disclosure; or (e) is subsequently and independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation of confidentiality and restriction on use in Section 2 hereof shall not apply to any Confidential Information that the Receiving Party proves: (a) was in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement or subsequently came into the public domain as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party; (b) was received by the Receiving Party from a third party free of any obligation of confidence of the Receiving Party to such third party; provided that such third party is not known by the Receiving Party, after reasonable diligence, to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other obligation of secrecy to the Disclosing Party; (c) was already in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party, without obligation of confidentiality, prior to receipt thereof, directly or indirectly, from the Disclosing Party; (d) is requested to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative law, regulation or proceeding after all reasonable legal remedies for maintaining such information in confidence have been exhausted, including, but not limited to, giving the Disclosing Party reasonable advance notice of the potential for such disclosure and allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order concerning such disclosure; or (e) is subsequently and independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4 originally excludes information that came into the public domain *other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party* from confidentiality obligations. I removed "other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party", meaning any information, even if disclosed by the Receiving Party, is no longer considered confidential. This directly contradicts section 2, which requires the receiving party to safeguard confidential information, and is not doing so.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly authorized by prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (a) limit access to any Confidential Information received by it solely to its Representatives who have a need to know such in connection with any current or future negotiations or arrangements between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party, including, without limitation in connection with the membership of the Investor in the Company and any commercial arrangements between the Investor and the Company and only for use in connection therewith. The Investor may provide access to and may disclose Confidential Information of the Company to any other Member of the Company in connection with such Member's participation as a Member of the Company. The Disclosing Party hereby consents to, and represents and warrants that it has obtained from its applicable Affiliates their consent to the use by the Receiving Party of any information received by the Receiving Party in connection with the above-referenced negotiations or arrangements; (b) advise its Representatives having access to the Confidential Information of the proprietary nature thereof and of the obligation set forth in this Agreement; (c) take appropriate action by instruction or agreement with its Representatives having access to the Confidential Information to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement; (d) safeguard all Confidential Information received by it using a reasonable degree of care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of such Confidential Information, but not less than that degree of care and discretion used by the Receiving Party in safeguarding its own similar information or material that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate; and (e) use all Confidential Information received by it for the purposes described in subsection (a) of this Section 2 and for no other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 4 to no longer exclude information disclosed by the Receiving Party from being considered non-confidential. This directly contradicts Section 2, which requires the Receiving Party to safeguard all confidential information. The removal of "other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party" in the changed text means that even information the Receiving Party discloses is no longer considered confidential, creating a conflict with the obligation to protect confidential information as outlined in Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: RIGHTS IN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does not confer any right, license, interest or title in, to or under the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. No license is hereby granted to the Receiving Party, by estoppel or otherwise, under any patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights of the Disclosing Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: RIGHTS IN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does confer a right for the Receiving Party to share Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to any 3rd party. No license is hereby granted to the Receiving Party, by estoppel or otherwise, under any patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights of the Disclosing Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Section 5 is modified to directly contradict the intent of the confidentiality agreement. It initially states that the agreement doesn't confer any right, license, interest or title in the disclosing party's confidential information to the receiving party. The modified version adds that the agreement *does* confer a right for the Receiving Party to share the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to any 3rd party. This completely undermines the confidentiality obligations outlined in section 2, as the receiving party is explicitly allowed to disclose the confidential information, contradicting the obligation to keep it confidential. The statement in the new definition contradicts with the whole concept of confidentiality of the rest of the legal text, and the rest of this section where "Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party."
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly authorized by prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (a) limit access to any Confidential Information received by it solely to its Representatives who have a need to know such in connection with any current or future negotiations or arrangements between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party, including, without limitation in connection with the membership of the Investor in the Company and any commercial arrangements between the Investor and the Company and only for use in connection therewith. The Investor may provide access to and may disclose Confidential Information of the Company to any other Member of the Company in connection with such Member's participation as a Member of the Company. The Disclosing Party hereby consents to, and represents and warrants that it has obtained from its applicable Affiliates their consent to the use by the Receiving Party of any information received by the Receiving Party in connection with the above-referenced negotiations or arrangements; (b) advise its Representatives having access to the Confidential Information of the proprietary nature thereof and of the obligation set forth in this Agreement; (c) take appropriate action by instruction or agreement with its Representatives having access to the Confidential Information to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement; (d) safeguard all Confidential Information received by it using a reasonable degree of care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of such Confidential Information, but not less than that degree of care and discretion used by the Receiving Party in safeguarding its own similar information or material that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate; and (e) use all Confidential Information received by it for the purposes described in subsection (a) of this Section 2 and for no other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement does not confer any right to the Receiving Party to Disclosing Party's Confidential Information. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that the agreement *does* confer a right for the Receiving Party to share the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to any 3rd party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the Receiving Party's obligations to keep the information confidential and limit its access and use as per Section 2. The change fundamentally undermines the confidentiality agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction, which adheres to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: EQUITABLE RELIEF. Each Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of the confidentiality and other obligations of this Agreement. Accordingly, in addition to all other remedies that each Party may have, each Party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief or other equitable relief as determined by a court of competent jurisdiction as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Each Party agrees to waive any requirement for a bond in connection with any such injunctive or other equitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: EQUITABLE RELIEF. Each Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for breach of the confidentiality and other obligations of this Agreement. Accordingly, in addition to all other remedies that each Party may have, each Party shall NOT be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief or other equitable relief as determined by a court of competent jurisdiction as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Each Party agrees to waive any requirement for a bond in connection with any such injunctive or other equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 7 the original states that each party *shall* be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief for breach of the agreement, I've negated this by having the parties *shall NOT* be entitled to that. This directly contradicts section 9 because, if a party breach this agreement, there can't be a remedy for enforcing compliance with the terms, such as an injunction to prevent disclosure or use of confidential information, as section 7 now explicitly prevents that. This undermines the entire basis of the confidentiality protections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be enforced by any Member of the Company with respect to any information or data regarding such Member disclosed by the Investor m violation of this Agreement. Except as set forth in the preceding sentence, nothing in this Agreement shall provide any benefit to any third party or entitle any third party to any claim, cause of action, remedy or right of any kind, it being the intent of the Parties that this Agreement shall not be construed as a third party beneficiary contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7 explicitly states that parties 'shall NOT' be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief. This directly contradicts Section 9, which implies the agreement can be enforced by a Member of the Company with respect to information disclosed by the Investor, suggesting injunctive relief could be a remedy. The change removes the possibility of enforcing compliance with the agreement terms, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
